source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage/Fire - Main Pass 73-A

event.top_event: Fire at gas compressor engine starter
actions_taken: Operator announced fire and sounded General Alarm
Four platform operators grabbed handheld fire extinguishers
Fire extinguished within approximately three minutes
Compressor locked out pending investigation and repairs
BSEE inspectors arrived on location for investigation
event.summary: On 27 January 2022 at approximately 1625 hours, a fire occurred near the starter of a rental gas compressor on Main Pass 73-A production platform. The fire resulted from an improperly tightened JIC fitting on the engine's starter exhaust hose that backed off over time due to vibration, combined with a leaking Flow Safety Valve (FSV) that allowed natural gas to migrate to the hot exhaust area (>900 degrees). Four platform operators successfully extinguished the fire using handheld fire extinguishers within three minutes. No injuries or pollution occurred. Damage was limited to compressor wiring and FSV internal parts, estimated at $12,000.

hazards.name: Pressurized natural gas in compressor system

threats.name: Improperly tightened JIC fitting backing off due to vibration
Leaking Flow Safety Valve allowing gas migration
Natural gas accumulation near hot exhaust

consequences.name: Damage to compressor wiring and equipment

controls.name: JIC fitting installation and tightening procedure
controls.barrier_role: Prevent gas leakage from exhaust hose
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent loose connections

supporting_text: Earlier maintenance performed on the compressor failed to properly tighten the JIC fitting which caused it to back off over time

Through Cox's investigation, it was discovered that the Joint Industry Council (JIC) fitting connecting the exhaust hose to the starter was most likely not completely tightened when it was last installed

controls.name: Flow Safety Valve (FSV) on exhaust line
controls.barrier_role: Prevent gas leakage from exhaust system
controls.lod_basis: Secondary barrier to contain gas if primary seal fails

supporting_text: The Flow Safety Valve (FSV) on the exhaust's line to the starter was also found to be leaking
The leaking FSV allowed small traces of natural gas to leak out and migrate to the turbo and exhaust area

controls.name: Temperature Safety Element (TSE) shutdown system
controls.barrier_role: Detect high temperature and shut down platform
controls.lod_basis: Detection and automatic shutdown to prevent escalation

supporting_text: The accumulated gas around the exhaust flashed off and melted the Temperature Safety Element (TSE), shutting in the platform
the production alarm sounded, and the platform shut in

controls.name: Handheld fire extinguishers and operator response
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to suppress active fire

supporting_text: Four platform operators in the area of the compressor grabbed handheld fire extinguishers and headed towards the fire
It took operators approximately three minutes to extinguish the fire from the time it was first observed
The four operators who extinguished the fire used two 30 pound ABC, and two 30 pound Purple K fire extinguishers to extinguish the fire

controls.name: Daily platform rounds and mechanical inspection
controls.barrier_role: Detect equipment issues before they escalate
controls.lod_basis: Preventive inspection to identify maintenance needs

supporting_text: On the morning of 27 January 2022, crew change took place, and platform operators and an onboard mechanic made their rounds around the platform to ensure there were no issues or threats to safety throughout the facility
During this time, the mechanic did not find any issues with the compressor

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
